weak_aliases.reason: M15+ (SBS variant) shares the M15 base name. The bare 'M15' label is a weak alias for search/discovery but the GTFS '+' suffix and sbs variant are the strong identity. Per canon: GTFS '+' ids are strong aliases for the SBS variant only on official GTFS/ACE surfaces.
The bare 'route_m15' key is shared across all M15 variants as a collision-avoidance artifact. Not a merge signal — variant is decisive.

Bare 'route_m15' and 'M15' keys are ambiguous across variants. This record's own description says 'M15 Local/Limited route reference' — a bundled category label.

missing_fields.note: Payload claims 'limited_stop' but description and source evidence bundle local/limited as a combined category. The record needs a definite variant pin or should remain as a bundled reference record.

needed_context: Clarify whether route_m15-local-limited is truly the limited_stop variant or a bundled local/limited reference. Its payload says 'limited_stop' but its description says 'M15 Local/Limited route reference' and its evidence (speeding_up_slowly_2025) discusses 'Local/Limited routes' as a combined MTA service category distinct from SBS and Express. If it is a bundled compound record, per canon it must not be folded into any single variant.
Same as above: resolve whether route_m15-local-limited's true variant is limited_stop or a bundled local/limited reference.

suggested_rules: When a route record's description explicitly acknowledges it bundles multiple variants (e.g. 'Local/Limited route reference'), the description should override a mechanically assigned payload variant for ambiguity purposes — the record should not merge into any single-variant group until the contradiction is resolved.
rationale: All records share base route M15 but fall into three distinct service variants: local (4 records), limited_stop (2 records), and sbs (1 record). Per Test 1 of the merge canon, different variants never merge. Within local: route_m15-cb8-2010, route_m15-local-2010-06-09-v2, route_m15-local-2010-09-14, and route_m15-local-20100607 all share same base + 'local' variant with only date/source suffixes — Test 2 merge. Within limited_stop: route_m15-limited-2010-06-09 and route_m15-limited-2010-09-14 share same base + variant with only date suffixes — Test 2 merge. route_m15-sbs stands alone as the sole SBS record (route_id 'M15+' per GTFS convention). route_m15-local-limited is ambiguous: payload says limited_stop but its own description admits it bundles 'Local/Limited' as a combined reference, and its supporting evidence cites the MTA's 'Local/Limited routes' service category alongside SBS and Express — not a specific variant pin. Validator duplicate_identity flags on shared keys like 'route_m15' and 'route_m15-local' are key-collision artifacts from pre-merge ingestion and are overridden by the variant-based identity rules.
